title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Program Brief: Validation Browser
summary: Tests snapshot everything, approve changed HTML blindly, omit malformed input, compare only strings, never mutate fixtures, and treat validator success as user-flow proof. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the validation browser increment in layered parser-to-browser verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.
Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Validation Browser, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered parser-to-browser verification matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_testing_validation_4(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-validation-browser-1wcj1y
# Competency: ssg-validation-browser.
# Case 1250: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_validation_browser_1wcj1y(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1wcj1y = "1wcj1y-y1jcw1-1250"
    assert evidence_variant_1wcj1y
    evidence_axes_1wcj1y = (
        "reader1wcj1y",
        "source1250",
        "parsey1jcw1",
        "route1wcj1y1250",
        "artifact12501wcj1y",
        "recoveryy1jcw11250",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1wcj1y)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Validation Browser model · Theory Validation Browser
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Validation Browser: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 8727 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Validation Browser model · Theory Validation Browser.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Validation Browser model · Theory Validation Browser, keep Validation Browser connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Validation Browser model · Theory Validation Browser, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Validation Browser precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Validation Browser model · Theory Validation Browser, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 8727 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Validation Browser model · Theory Validation Browser, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Valid HTML proves routes, interactions, and reader tasks work.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-validation-browser-1wcj1y.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-validation-browser-1wcj1y(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_validation_browser_1wcj1y\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?detected_mutants < required_mutants)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?validator_errors > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?browser_tasks_failed > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
